qa_pairs.question: Where can I find official documentation for creating Heat Orchestration Template (HOT) files?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find official documentation for creating Heat Orchestration Template (HOT) files at http://docs.openstack.org/developer/heat/template_guide/hot_guide.html.

qa_pairs.question: What format are Heat Orchestration Template (HOT) files written in?
qa_pairs.answer: Heat Orchestration Template (HOT) files are written in the YAML format.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of automation does Heat provide for the OpenStack dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: Heat allows for the automation of operations performed in the OpenStack dashboard (Horizon).

qa_pairs.question: Can Heat pass information to embedded CloudInit files?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Heat can pass information into embedded CloudInit files, such as the IP of another server.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary to create resources in advance before using a Heat template?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is usually no need to create any resources in advance before using a Heat template.

qa_pairs.question: Why should I remove unused resources before using a Heat template?
qa_pairs.answer: It is good practice to remove any resources you are not currently using beforehand because using a Heat template consumes resources towards your quota and will fail if it tries to exceed your quota.

qa_pairs.question: How do I start the process of creating a new stack using a HOT file?
qa_pairs.answer: To start creating a new stack using a HOT file, select ''Project''->''Orchestration''->''Stacks'' and click the ''Launch Stack'' button.

qa_pairs.question: What are the ways to provide a Heat Orchestration Template (HOT) file when creating a stack?
qa_pairs.answer: You can provide a HOT file by entering its URL, specifying the file name, or by using Direct Input.

qa_pairs.question: When providing a HOT file via a URL, what steps should I take in the ''Template Source'' box?
qa_pairs.answer: In the ''Template Source'' box, select ''URL'' from the drop-down list and then paste the selected URL into the ''Template URL'' box.

qa_pairs.question: What is the next step after providing the HOT file URL when creating a stack?
qa_pairs.answer: After providing the HOT file URL, click ''Next'' to begin setting stack parameters.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default parameters for all stacks?
qa_pairs.answer: All stacks have the following parameters by default: ''Stack Name'', ''Creation Timeout'', and ''Password for user''.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the ''Stack Name'' parameter when creating a stack?
qa_pairs.answer: The ''Stack Name'' parameter allows you to choose a meaningful name for your stack.

qa_pairs.question: What does the ''Creation Timeout'' parameter indicate?
qa_pairs.answer: The ''Creation Timeout'' parameter indicates how long after stack creation OpenStack will give up trying to create the stack if it hasn't finished; the default value is usually sufficient.

qa_pairs.question: What is the function of the ''Password for user'' parameter?
qa_pairs.answer: The ''Password for user'' parameter sets the password required for later stack changes, though it is seldom used as many stacks are not designed to be updated.

qa_pairs.question: How can I visually monitor the progress of my stack creation?
qa_pairs.answer: To graphically see the progress of your stack creation, click on the ''Stack Name'' and select the ''Topology'' tab.

qa_pairs.question: In the ''Topology'' tab, what do gray, green, and red nodes signify?
qa_pairs.answer: In the ''Topology'' tab, gray nodes indicate that creation is in progress, green nodes have finished being created, and red nodes indicate failures.

qa_pairs.question: After a stack has successfully completed, where can I find information provided by the stack?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the stack has completed successfully, click the ''Overview'' tab to see any information it may provide, such as a URL to access a service or website.
